With multiple bids in hand, the NBA now plans to select those it considers the best, using input from its Board of Governors. Because of that process, teams in the European league are expected to be announced in stages, instead of all at once.

The NBA has long wanted a permanent foothold in Europe, going back to the 1980s and the tenure of former Commissioner David Stern. The league believes basketball’s popularity in Europe — its data suggests there are 270 million basketball fans there — is a largely untapped business opportunity, with large markets such as London and Rome devoid of top-flight basketball teams.

Stern pushed for NBA players to take part in the 1992 Olympics in Barcelona. The league took teams abroad for exhibitions against European clubs. Eventually Stern’s successor, Adam Silver, began hosting regular-season NBA games on the continent, too, with stops in Berlin and London as recently as January.

Yet the proposed league, which is also backed by FIBA, international basketball’s governing body, is not intended to act as an easternmost conference of the current 30-team NBA, which is mulling its own domestic expansion in Las Vegas and Seattle.

Instead, the NBA’s European league would be its own, distinct entity, anchored by 10 to 12 permanent members, with the remaining league slots earned by teams that qualify through other competitions, similar to European soccer leagues. At a March meeting of the NBA’s Board of Governors, Silver said 12 cities were being targeted for the launch but did not go into detail. Currently, the league is focused on placing teams in London, Manchester, Paris, Lyon, Madrid, Barcelona, Rome, Milan, Berlin, Munich, Athens and Istanbul.

That doesn’t mean the NBA and its European cousin would be entirely separated. The NBA has already looked at creating competitions that could pit NBA teams against their European counterparts, said one person with knowledge of plans. Tatum, the NBA’s deputy commissioner, last fall described a “preseason cup” as one hypothetical possibility in the short term.

“Five, 10 years down the road, you could see a situation where the winners of the top two finishers in the European league get entered into the NBA Cup tournament,” Tatum told Sports Business Journal.

The NBA’s in-season cup tournament, which began in 2023, drew inspiration from European soccer, whose popularity in the U.S. has partly factored into the league’s belief that now is the right time to launch a league on the continent. Despite the time difference, U.S. fans have gotten used to watching England’s Premier League on weekends, plus European competitions during the week.

The geographic divide also hasn’t limited interest in the NBA from abroad. European-raised NBA superstars such as Giannis Antetokounmpo, Luka Doncic and Nikola Jokic led to record viewership numbers for the NBA in Europe last season.

The European cities the NBA has targeted come with complications. Though Manchester and London have arenas that would meet the league’s state-of-the-art standard, others would likely need renovations.
Not all cities currently have top-flight basketball clubs, either, which is why any new European league would require some clubs founded from scratch.

The NBA has also sought to determine whether established, high-profile soccer clubs in some of their desired markets would be open to adding a basketball team. And while some cities do have established and high-profile clubs, they currently play in EuroLeague, a closed league of 20 teams including such powers as Real Madrid and Barcelona.

The bids that arrived by Tuesday’s deadline followed discussions in recent weeks between the NBA and more than a dozen existing European basketball teams about potential inclusion. Among those interested were teams that currently play in the EuroLeague, one of the people said. Under the NBA and FIBA’s proposal, the only way a team can guarantee a permanent place in their European league would be to go through its investment process.
The NBA and EuroLeague are expected to resume talks soon that would explore a potential collaboration, rather than a collision.

“I think for the betterment of European basketball, the best outcome would be if we came together with the EuroLeague here and that we came up with a systematic approach to growing the game throughout Europe,” Silver told reporters last month.

EuroLeague had taken a defiant tone as the NBA’s interest in Europe became public; in January, it reportedly warned the NBA of legal action if it spoke to EuroLeague-affiliated clubs. But in March, EuroLeague hired Chus Bueno, a new chief executive with a more conciliatory stance. He previously worked as an executive in the NBA league office for 12 years.

“If they raise the money, the most logical thing would be a joint competition,” Bueno told Spain’s Mundo Deportivo in March. “Having two top-level competitions would split the market and lower the average level. It wouldn’t help anyone. The best thing is to do it together, even if it means compromises from both sides.”

title: Pete Hegseth has intervened in military promotions for more than a dozen senior officers
summary: WASHINGTON — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has taken steps to block or delay promotions for more than a dozen Black and female senior officers across all four branches of the military, some of whom are seen as having been targeted because of their race, gender or perceived affiliation with Biden administration policies or officials, according to nine U.S. officials familiar with the process
story: WASHINGTON — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has taken steps to block or delay promotions for more than a dozen Black and female senior officers across all four branches of the military, some of whom are seen as having been targeted because of their race, gender or perceived affiliation with Biden administration policies or officials, according to nine U.S. officials familiar with the process.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The process within the Army, the Air Force, the Navy and the Marines is structured to ensure the most qualified officers get promoted. Hegseth’s decision to intervene in the process has raised concerns among some officials within those military branches and the White House, the nine U.S. officials familiar with the situation said.
“There is not a single service that has been immune to this level of involvement by Hegseth,” one of the U.S. officials said.

Two of the officials said there are concerns in the military and the White House specifically that Hegseth is blocking or stalling some qualified officers from receiving promotions through the ranks of general and admiral because of their race or gender as he targets diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives at the Pentagon. There is also concern that Hegseth could be singling out military officers whom he views as aligned with officials or policies of the Biden administration, the officials said.

On Thursday Hegseth fired the Army chief of staff, Gen. Randy George, whose term was expected to be four years ending in September 2027. George, the Army’s top officer, was senior military assistant to Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin during the Biden administration.

George recently asked to meet with Hegseth to discuss Hegseth's blocking of promotions for some Army officers, which seemed to focus on women and Black men, but Hegseth refused to meet or discuss his decisions, according to two additional U.S. officials.

Hegseth, who declared an end to “woke” culture at the Defense Department last year, has criticized DEI initiatives adopted by the Biden administration, as has President Donald Trump. Hegseth also has publicly accused the military of awarding promotions based on diversity rather than merit. Not all promotions for officers who are women or members of racial minority groups have been blocked or delayed during his tenure as secretary, four of the U.S. officials familiar with the process said.

The U.S. military branches either did not respond to requests for comment or referred questions to the Pentagon. A spokesperson for the Defense Department did not respond to multiple requests for comment for this article.
The White House also did not respond to multiple requests for comment.

Two officials said that among the attributes Hegseth has cited for removing officers from promotions are past support for Covid vaccines or mask mandates for troops, which were enacted during the Biden administration, or whether officers were affiliated with DEI programs, potentially being women or members of racial minority groups, or promoted or worked on such initiatives.

The officials said an officer’s association with former Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman Mark Milley, whom Trump views as a political enemy, can also make an officer who is up for a promotion susceptible to scrutiny from Hegseth’s office.
“I think there is not consistency being applied to the standards” for promotions, another of the U.S. officials said.

In recent weeks, Hegseth blocked three Marine officers who were expected to be promoted or appointed to new roles — two women and a Black man — according to five of the U.S. officials familiar with the decision. None of the Marines were the subjects of internal investigations that might raise concerns about moving forward with their promotions, according to two of the officials. Marine Corps leaders recommended their promotions, but Hegseth refused to allow their selections to move forward despite attempts by the corps’ leadership to intervene, the two officials said.

A list of naval officers who had been selected to be promoted to one-star admiral has been held up for more than a month as it sits on Hegseth’s desk, according to three of the officials. A list of promotions typically would move through the process more quickly, according to the three officials who expressed concern that some of the Navy officers on the list could be removed because of their race or gender.
Hegseth has also blocked promotions for a handful of more senior naval officers, the three officials said.
Other officers have been pulled off a list of Air Force promotions at the direction of Hegseth’s office, according to two of the officials familiar with the process.
Some of the naval and Air Force officers whose promotions have been blocked are also women or members of racial minority groups, according to officials.

When it decides promotions, each branch of the military typically convenes a board, which comprises service members from that branch, to review possible candidates to advance. The boards select candidates to move up in rank, and lists are created for promotions to one-star and two-star general and admiral.
Those lists are sent to the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and the defense secretary’s office and then to the White House. Ultimately, the Senate votes on the lists for each military branch.

Promotions to three-star and four-star general and admiral are typically conducted individually, not via lists, but they also are sent from each military branch to the Joint Chiefs chairman and the defense secretary’s office, then to the White House and to the Senate for votes.
Defense secretaries typically do not remove officers from promotion lists or reject individual recommendations from the service branches, according to three U.S. officials familiar with the process.

By law the president has the most authority to block a military promotion, either an individual recommendation or a name on a list. If a recommended promotion is pulled before it is transmitted to the White House, a reason must be provided, such as an ongoing investigation or an allegation about an officer’s conduct, and the defense secretary typically does not make those decisions. Candidates for promotion have been removed in the past if there were allegations against them or military investigations into them. The people removed from promotion lists did not have open investigations against them, U.S. officials said.

The Army’s list of promotions this year included about 30 officers to become one-star generals and went to the White House around mid-March, then to the Senate on Monday, but only after Hegseth struck the four names from it, according to five U.S. officials.

While Hegseth removed two women and two Black officers from the Army’s list, the list that progressed to the Senate did include some women and racial minority officers, according to the five officials, who said they believe race and gender played a role in Hegseth’s removal of the other officers.

“If there are no open allegations or investigations, what was the reason they were removed from the list?” one of the officials said. “They have all deployed and done their jobs, and all are all combat-tested.”
The New York Times first reported Hegseth was blocking promotions for female or minority officers in the Army.

According to Defense Department data from 2024, the most recent available, 80% of active-duty members of the military are male. Overall, 67% are white; 18% are Black. Roughly 80% of active-duty officers are male. Overall, 74% of officers are white; only 9% are Black.

During Hegseth’s tenure, several top military officers have been removed, including former Joint Chiefs Chairman CQ Brown Jr., a Black man, and former Chief of Naval Operations Lisa Franchetti, a white woman. From the perspective of Hegseth and his staff, they were not aligned with the Trump administration’s priorities, according to two of the U.S. officials.
A retired senior military officer described the promotion process as rigorous and said any meddling by the defense secretary could diminish trust in it.

“Our officer corps trusts our promotion process,” the retired officer said, adding that intervention in the process without an explanation “will certainly cast a shadow across our officer corps that everything they have said, done and written about during their careers could be politicized in a career-ending manner with the stroke of a pen.”

title: FBI labels suspected China hack of law enforcement data 'a major cyber incident'
summary: The FBI has labeled a suspected Chinese cyber intrusion into a government surveillance system a “major incident” that poses risks to U.S. national security, according to a senior law enforcement official and a source with knowledge of the matter
story: The FBI has labeled a suspected Chinese cyber intrusion into a government surveillance system a “major incident” that poses risks to U.S. national security, according to a senior law enforcement official and a source with knowledge of the matter.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The hack compromised sensitive information related to domestic law enforcement, the sources said, and the FBI recently informed lawmakers about it.

The revelation suggested a significant U.S. counterintelligence failure amid repeated hacking operations by China-linked actors in recent years that have penetrated critical infrastructure and telecommunications firms, former officials said.
The FBI and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency declined to comment.
The declaration by the FBI of a major cyber incident was first reported by Politico.

Under federal data security laws, a cyber breach is declared a “major incident” only if it involves the compromise of personally identifiable information that could cause “demonstrable harm” to national security interests, foreign relations, the economy, civil liberties or the public health of Americans.”

The cyber intrusion appeared to use similar tactics and techniques employed by a Chinese hacking effort known as Salt Typhoon, which penetrated major telecommunications providers in an unprecedented breach, according to the source with knowledge of the matter.
The hackers in Salt Typhoon, which was uncovered in 2024, were able to obtain phone call records from millions of Americans and steal FBI wiretap data.

The Salt Typhoon hacking campaign was one of the largest intelligence compromises in American history. It breached eight domestic telecom and internet service providers and dozens of others around the world. U.S. officials said in 2024 that victims included people in both major parties’ presidential campaigns.

Under the 1994 Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act (CALEA), American telecoms are required to maintain systems to surveil unencrypted conversations when required by court order. Salt Typhoon accessed some CALEA systems in 2024, officials told NBC News.
China has denied responsibility for Salt Typhoon.

The breach of law enforcement data showed that Chinese-backed hackers were continuing to target the U.S. despite the global attention Salt Typhoon generated and attempts by the Trump administration to lower tensions ahead of President Donald Trump’s scheduled visit to Beijing next month, a former senior cybersecurity official said.
“Their hacking continues with impunity,” the former official said. “This shows they don’t feel deterred by the larger global exposure. ... They’re bold.”
Sen. Mark Warner, D-Va., the vice chair of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said the episode illustrated the persistent threat posed by China and other sophisticated cyber adversaries.
“From Salt Typhoon to Stryker to now this reported breach at the FBI, the pattern is clear: our adversaries are probing for weaknesses, and they’re finding them,” Warner said in a statement.
He added that major cutbacks to cybersecurity staff across the government were putting the country’s digital defenses at risk.

“What makes this even more concerning is that, at the very moment these threats are escalating, this administration has been systematically hollowing out the very cyber expertise we rely on to defend the country, pushing out experienced professionals at the FBI and at Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency,” he said.
The former cybersecurity official said U.S. adversaries are keenly aware that major reductions in the federal workforce provide a valuable opportunity to probe computer networks.

“They’ve got awareness at this point that the federal government is fairly hollowed out. If you’re an adversary thinking about intelligence collection, this is certainly a time to take advantage of this opportunity,” the former official said.

Cynthia Kaiser, who left a role as a senior official in the FBI’s cyber division in May, told NBC News that while China had been targeting American communications for several years, this intrusion was discovered after she left the agency.

“China is consistently targeting any information that can help them identify and track their own targets, communication and movements around the world. And intelligence community, FBI and law enforcement intercepts would absolutely help them meet these goals,” she said.

title: 'Love on the Spectrum' Couple Madison Marilla and Tyler White Just Got Engaged
summary: Where are Madison Marilla and Tyler White from Love on the Spectrum now?
story: Madison Marilla is taking the next step in her relationship with her "cowboy sweetheart," Tyler White.
The couple, who met while filming Season 3 of "Love on the Spectrum," get engaged in Season 4 of the hit Netflix reality series.
In Episode 6, Tyler planed a romantic, Christmas-themed anniversary date for Madison, who loves the holiday.
During the date, the couple exchanged gifts then Tyler handed Madison a letter.

"I knew in my heart when I met you that I'd found someone special, unique and precious. With this being our one-year anniversary, I would like to give you a gift to express just how much you mean to me," the letter read. "From the moment I met you, I knew I had met my person and I don't want to wait another moment to let the world know just how I feel. We can call it Christmas in July from now until forever."
After reciting a Bible verse, Tyler got down on one knee and proposed. As he spoke, Madison kept repeating, "I can't believe this!"
Tyler opened a jewelry box containing a ring that appeared to have been inspired by a candy cane since it features rubies and diamonds.
The newly engaged couple embraced then Madison excitedly started planning their wedding.
In the season finale, the couple welcomed many of their "Love on the Spectrum" co-stars and friends to their engagement party.

Madison and Tyler haven't posted about their engagement on their Instagram pages yet, but many viewers of "Love on the Spectrum" are already sharing their well wishes for the couple on their other recent posts.
"Just finished e6, I am SOBBING🥹🥹🥹 I am so happy for you and Tyler, congratulations and so many well wishes," one commented.
"You looked just like a Princess saying YES to your Cowboy sweetheart," another wrote.
One Instagram user wrote, "So happy you’re engaged! And seeing the party with everyone together got me a little emotional."
Another commented, "I cried all the way through! You are all amazing humans & I wish you all the happiness!"
What Madison and Tyler Have Shared About Their Engagement
In an interview with Netflix's Tudum, Madison said she and Tyler are "very happily in love."
"Being engaged has made us so excited to plan our future together," she said.
Madison said she had a feeling that Tyler was planning a surprise for her before he proposed.

"Tyler pulled my parents aside, and they were speaking for a very long time when we all had dinner at Tyler’s house," she said. "I kind of had a feeling that night that he was probably asking them for their blessing."
What Madison and Tyler Have Shared About Their Wedding Plans
Looking ahead to their wedding, Madison wants to wear a "good ole princess ball gown" and Tyler plans to wear his cowboy hat, she told Tudum.
During an interview with Entertainment Tonight, Madison revealed that she and Tyler haven't started wedding planning just yet.
"We've had to keep the engagement a secret. Plus, we definitely want to take our time to work through all the process and speed bumps," Tyler said.
"Yes, we want to get married when the time is right. There's no rush to getting married and planning a wedding. We want to do it on our time and on God's time," Madison said.
In an interview with People, the engaged couple said they see each other "daily" and like to attend church together on Sundays.

"We go on lots of dates," Tyler said. "There have been times we’d go get something quick to eat and then go see a movie after church on Sunday, and I get her for Wednesday night. We go eat and then go to church and I take her back home."

title: Jenna Bush Hager Gives an Update After Her Fall on Live TV, Knowing ‘Some of You Were Worried’
summary: Jenna Bush Hager gave an update as to how she was doing after her fall on live TV.
story: Jenna Bush Hager is doing just fine after she fell while playing an April Fools' prank on Willie Geist when he filled in as guest co-host during the April Fool’s Day episode of TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.

During the episode, Jenna and Willie were speaking with TODAY digital executive editor Arianna Davis about topics heating up the Internet when they brought out a viral cake that supposedly cost $20,000. When Jenna took the cake, she fell and dropped it, slipping on icing and falling to the ground herself in the process.
Willie quickly realized it was a gag, but he didn’t try to help Jenna get up.

“I thought the fall was part of it, and I didn’t even move to help my friend off the ground, and I’m really sorry about that,” he said while discussing the joke during the April 2 episode of TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.
“Evidently, some of you were worried about my body parts, and at this old age, you should,” she said, while Willie pointed out her knee.
Jenna said, however, that she is totally fine before pointing out her kids said she got what she deserved for playing the joke.

“I caught myself, so no harm,” she said. “I don’t even have a bruise, nope. But the fall was real, and I think it’s a good time to tell you that if you accidentally put cake on the ground, icing is quite slippery. And I showed it to my kids, and you know what they said, ‘That’s karma, bro.”
Willie said he received quite the response to the prank.
“That was amazing, and I heard about it all day. Like, that broke away from even the TODAY show world. I was hearing from people who just picked it up on their phones,” Willie said.

Jenna said “we have to do something” for an April Fools' prank. Willie said he “bought” the concept of the $20,000 cake when it was discussed in a meeting and then again when it was brought out to Jenna on the set during the show. But he realized soon enough there was some tomfoolery going on.

“The minute I realized it wasn’t real was when you dumped it so quickly,” he said about Jenna dropping the cake, while pointing out that he looked away from her and didn’t help her because he thought the fall was also part of the joke.
Even Sharon Stone, who was in the studio to be on the show, was worried about Jenna.

“Our amazing crew ... had to clean up a spilled cake, get a carpet down and a couch,” he said. And then Sharon Stone leaned in, and she’s like, ‘Jenna, are you OK? Like, she was genuinely concerned you had injured yourself.”
“She was like, ‘I have worries you are injured,’” Jenna recalled. “And I’m like, ‘No, no, luckily, I’m not.’ I’m like, ‘I’m just humiliated.’”

title: Lara Jean Is Back! Lana Condor's Role in 'XO, Kitty' Season 3, Explained
summary: "XO, Kitty" fans are looking forward to a long-awaited cameo from Lana Condor in Season 3. Here's what happened.
story: Warning: This article contains spoilers from Season 3 of "XO, Kitty."
"XO, Kitty" fans are looking forward to a long-awaited cameo from Lana Condor in Season 3.

The actor, who played Lara Jean Song-Covey in the "To All the Boys I've Loved Before" franchise, visits her onscreen sister Kitty (Anna Cathcart) at KISS (Korean Independent School of Seoul) and appears in a few episodes of Season 3.
Netflix shared the news in March and posted a video of Condor and Cathcart on set together. In the short clip, Min Ho (Sang Heon Lee) turns and says, "Covey?"
Both Condor and Cathcart turn, and Condor says, "Yes?"
Condor also commented on the post, writing, "HI SISTERRR."

While appearing on the 3rd hour of TODAY March 23, Condor spoke about Season 3 of “XO, Kitty.” She praised Cathcart's talents on the show, saying, "It’s really her own space, and I was just a guest there."
"To be honest, it's just a love letter to the fans," Condor said. “All the time I’m asked, ‘Are you going to go back?’ And I had to lie for so long. So now it’s nice to be able to tell people."
Last season, “XO, Kitty” fans were treated to cameos from two beloved characters from the “To All the Boys I’ve Loved Before” movies.
Lara Jean’s boyfriend, Peter, played by Noah Centineo, visited Kitty in South Korea in Season 2 and delighted viewers when they learned that the couple was still going strong.
Kitty’s older sister Margot (Janel Parrish) also flew to South Korea to support her as she arranged a reunion with a long-lost relative of their family.
So what happens when Lara Jean finally appears in "XO, Kitty"?
Lana Condor’s Role In 'XO, Kitty' Season 3, Explained

At the end of Episode 1, Kitty gets a call from Lara Jean, but viewers don’t hear what she says. In Episode 2, viewers learn that Lara Jean is going through a rough patch with Peter, and she asks Kitty to join her in New York City for the summer.
Lara Jean explains that her life isn’t compatible with Peter’s at the moment since he has a job in California and she’s pursuing a career in publishing in New York City.
"You're gonna figure this out with Peter. I just know it," Kitty says.
Kitty then tells Lara Jean the distance won't matter once she publishes her first novel, which Kitty snuck a peek at, unbeknownst to Lara Jean.
Kitty then reveals that she's fallen in love with New York University and plans to apply to the school.
"So if I go to NYC and you two make up and move in together, I will be over every Sunday night for dinner. Fear not," Kitty says.
Kitty, who's unsure where things stand with her and Min Ho after they spent the summer apart, also gets some relationship and life advice from Lara Jean.
"Whatever happens with Min Ho, I think it's important that you make sure you take the time to discover who you are. Just make senior year count," she says.
Flash forward to Episode 6. After officially starting a relationship at the start of their senior year at KISS, Min Ho and Kitty break up after yet another misunderstanding.
So Lara Jean surprises Kitty with a visit to South Korea to cheer her up.
Lara Jean encourages Kitty to work on her NYU early admission application. While doing some sightseeing together, Lara Jean also provides an update on her relationship with Peter.
"We are not back together, but Peter is coming to New York just to talk. We are still 5,000 miles apart literally and emotionally," she says.
"Well, I'm not gonna give up hope. I know you two are end game," Kitty says.
"I don't know about that. But I will keep you posted," Lara Jean says.

Later in the episode, the sisters dine out with their cousin Jiwon (Hojo Shin) and discover that she's pregnant. They watch from afar as she tells Alex (Peter Thurnwald) that he's the father of her child.
As Jiwon and Alex, two of Kitty's matchmaking targets earlier in the season, celebrate their news, Lara Jean takes a moment to reflect on the life Kitty has built in South Korea.
"I think if mom could see you now, she'd be so proud of you. I know I am," she says.

After reading Kitty's NYU application essay, Lara Jean realizes her sister seems hesitant about attending the school. Kitty explains that she's still hoping she will get back together with Min Ho and is nervous about leaving behind her life in South Korea.

"I don't know what's gonna happen between you and Min Ho. I don't know what's gonna happen between me and Peter. But we can't just stop living our lives. I think that we have to follow our hearts and trust that it will lead us to our next great adventure," she says.
As she's saying goodbye to Kitty, Lara Jean reveals she submitted her manuscript. Kitty is psyched and says, "I can't believe I'm gonna be related to a New York Times bestselling author."
After Kitty leaves for class, Lara Jean spots Min Ho outside. He comes over, says hello and asks how Kitty is doing.

"Honestly, she's great. You know, I thought I was coming here to help her and as it turns out at the end of this she's the one who inspired me," Lara Jean says. "She's a really great person to have in your corner."
"Yeah, she is," he replies.
"Just don't forget that," she says.
Finally in Episode 8, the Season 3 finale, Lara Jean calls to wish Kitty happy birthday and says her boss loved her manuscript. She also says that Peter says hi, implying that they're back together.
"Obviously there's a lot of things we have to figure out. But we are gonna make it work. Love is worth the risk, right?" she says.

title: What 'Michael' Star Jaafar Jackson Remembers About His Uncle, Michael Jackson
summary: Who is Jafaar Jackson? The actor play his uncle, Michael Jackson, in the upcoming biopic "Michael." Here's how they are related.
story: The Jackson family name will certainly be represented when “Michael” comes out later this month.
The movie chronicles the life of superstar Michael Jackson, who died in 2009 at the age of 50.

Jackson was one of the world’s most famous people for decades, so finding the right actor to play him was key. Director Antoine Fuqua didn’t have to go too far to land the person he thought fit the bill: The “Billie Jean” hitmaker’s own nephew, Jafaar Jackson, will portray him in his first onscreen acting role.

“It was a process that I really had to earn, and it really proved to the filmmakers and myself and my family that I can get to that point where I can pull it off,” he said April 2 on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle in what was his first television interview.
Jafaar Jackson also shared memories of spending time with Michael when he was younger.

“I remember just growing up, and we would have family time. Michael would come over to where I was growing up ... and we’d have some some days where we’d all get together, play family game day,” he said. “We’d have wonderful times at Neverland and just playing hide-and-go-seek, getting as much candy, going on rides, watching movies. It was all fun.”

“There's definitely a responsibility that you feel going into something this big, and the scope, and to tell it in a cinematic way. There's so many moments that I wanted to nail and make sure I had the true essence and that feeling. I had to feel it to make sure I believed in it, first,” he continued.
Michael Jackson’s mother, Katherine Jackson, had given the seal of approval to the casting when it was announced in 2023.
“Jaafar embodies my son. It’s so wonderful to see him carry on the Jackson legacy of entertainers and performers,” she said in a press release.
Jaafar Jackson told TODAY the support from his family has been "heartwarming."
“I honestly wouldn't be able to pull this off if I didn't have the support,” he said.
Michael Jackson’s daughter, Paris Jackson, has been critical of the film, saying she wasn’t involved and that it has some inaccuracies.
“Michael,” which also stars Colman Domingo, Nia Long, Miles Teller, Kendrick Sampson and Laren Tate, opens April 24.
Read on to find out more about Jackson and just how he is related to the King of Pop.
Who Is Jaafar Jackson?
Jafaar Jackson is a singer and dancer, whose 2019 song “Got Me Singing” has garnered more than 10 million views on YouTube.
He has also appeared in a 2015 episode of the reality show “The Jacksons: Next Generation” and in the 2021 music video for uncle Tito Jackson’s single “Love One Another.”
The younger Jackson, who says the vocals in the film are a mixture of his and his uncle’s voices, also wanted to make sure he gave Michael the kind of attention to detail he deserved.

“I wanted to set goals for myself to nail the mannerisms. It was the vocal of it was just his essence and and how he goes about in a conversational way. But I wanted to make sure I had everything in a balanced way  — I didn’t want the dancing to outdo the acting and the acting outdo the dancing,” he said.
“I wanted to really embody it completely and do it in a way where it feels natural, like it feels like I’m a seasoned actor, since it’s my first time, but I wanted to approach it in that sense.”
How Are Jaafar Jackson and Michael Jackson Related?
Jaafar Jackson is Michael Jackson’s nephew. Jafaar Jackson’s father, Jermaine Jackson, is is Michael’s brother.
His mother is Alejandra Oaziaza, who also has two kids with Jermaine and Michael’s brother, Randy Jackson.
What Has Jaafar Jackson Said About Michael Jackson?
After his casting was announced, Jafaar Jackson reflected on the responsibility of playing his uncle.
“I’m humbled and honored to bring my Uncle Michael’s story to life. To all the fans all over the world, I’ll see you soon,” he captioned a picture on Instagram of what appeared to be him rehearsing

“I never dreamed to be an actor, or even thought of playing him, but I knew it was a calling,” he said. “Embodying Michael, I really had to earn the role and prove to the filmmakers that I was capable of becoming Michael.”

title: Meryl Streep and Anna Wintour Are Related
summary: Meryl Streep’s “The Devil Wears Prada” character Miranda Priestly is thought to be inspired by former Vogue editor-in-chief Anna Wintour.
story: Meryl Streep’s “The Devil Wears Prada” character Miranda Priestly is thought to be inspired by former Vogue editor-in-chief Anna Wintour. But it turns out the connection between the two goes deeper than an iconic role and impeccable taste.

Ahead of Streep reprising her role as the head of the fictional Runway magazine for “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” arriving in theaters May 1, Ancestry discovered that the three-time Oscar winner and the fashion mogul are related.
The genealogy company has confirmed that Streep and Wintour share fifth great-grandparents, Thomas Smith and Elizabeth Kinsey, making them sixth cousins, TODAY exclusively revealed April 2.

Smith and Kinsey resided in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, Ancestry also shared, which happens to be just 20 miles from where “The Devil Wears Prada” author, Lauren Weisberger, was raised. Weisberger previously worked as Wintour’s assistant before penning her debut novel.

Streep and Wintour have crossed paths multiple times since “The Devil Wears Prada” was released in 2006. Over a decade after Streep first portrayed Miranda Priestly, she met up with Wintour for an interview in her Vogue office. The magazine shared a video of their 2017 sit-down, which featured an intro similar to Miranda Priestly’s dramatic arrival at the beginning of the 2006 flick.

At the time of the video, Streep was starring in the movie “The Post,” in which she played the late Washington Post publisher Katharine Graham. Wintour described Graham as her friend in the clip before asking the actor to name the most challenging woman she has ever played.
“Hmm … oh I should say!” Streep began before Wintour laughed and cut her off.
Wintour playfully said, “No, we’re not going there, Meryl.”
The two reunited in September 2025 when they both attended the Dolce & Gabbana Milan Fashion Week show while Streep was filming “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” Variety reported.

Vogue uploaded a video of the interaction on Instagram that showed the two embracing while both wearing sunglasses indoors. The caption read, in part, “Can you please spell Gabbana,” a line Anne Hathaway’s character Andy Sachs asks in the first movie.

“This is my first fashion show,” Streep told Wintour in the clip. Wintour later chatted with Stanley Tucci, who is reprising his role of Nigel Kipling in the sequel, as well as Simone Ashley, who is playing a new character.
A couple weeks before their reunion, Wintour was a guest on “The New Yorker Radio Hour” podcast and revealed how she feels about the comparisons between her and Miranda Priestly.

The Condé Nast global chief content officer and artistic director recalled attending the premiere for “The Devil Wears Prada” 20 years ago while wearing Prada, and “having no idea what the film was going to be about.”
“I think that the fashion industry were very sweetly concerned for me about the film. That it was going to paint me in some kind of difficult light,” she remembered.
Host David Remnick said those close to her might have been worried the portrayal could be “cartoonish.”
Wintour agreed and said, “Yes, a caricature.”

“But first of all it was Meryl Streep, which, fantastic,” she continued. “Then I went to see the film, and I found it highly enjoyable and very funny. Miuccia (Prada) and I talk about it a lot. I say to her, ‘Well it was really good for you.’ You can imagine what she says back.”
She appreciated the film’s humor and wit, and Streep and Emily Blunt’s performances.
“They were all amazing. In the end, I thought it was a fair shot,” Wintour said.

title: Theresa DeMaria from 'Age of Attraction' Explains What Happened Between Her and John
summary: Sparks flew when “Age of Attraction” stars Theresa DeMaria and John Merrill met, but one major obstacle stood in their way: a 27-year age difference
story: Sparks flew when “Age of Attraction” stars Theresa DeMaria and John Merrill met, but one major obstacle stood in their way: a 27-year age difference.
Theresa, 54, didn't realize that her new beau was 27 years old until they decided to commit to each other early on in the dating experiment.

After moving in together, reality set in and the couple had to navigate the opinions of their family and friends. Theresa was  concerned about how her children, who are close in age to John, would react.
Like other "Age of Attraction" couples, Theresa and John spent a few weeks building their connection in the real world then had to decide whether they'd stay together or not.

In the Season 1 finale, Theresa kept John (and viewers) guessing as she weighed the pros and cons of staying together. The mother of three tells TODAY.com she wasn't "100% sure" of what her decision would be when she met up with John that day.
"I was probably leaning more towards, ‘Yes, I want to continue,’” she says. “I had without a doubt started to develop feelings for John."
Theresa says she was relying on John to give her the “reassurance” she needed in that moment before she made her final decision.

“I could see that he was definitely distraught and upset when he saw me sort of wavering, and that bothered me because obviously I’m very empathetic and I don’t want to hurt anybody. And I could feel how much he did really care for me and how much he really wanted to move forward. So that was kind of the reassurance that I got,” she says.
After reading John a letter that her children had written him, Theresa "solidified" her decision to move forward with their relationship.
What happened after the show stopped filming and where does their relationship stand today? Theresa's filling us in below.
Why Did Theresa and John Break Up?
After committing to each other in the Season 1 finale, Theresa and John broke up in the fall of 2025.
"We're not together romantically right now. We ended up breaking up last fall," she says.
Theresa says "communication issues" were at the center of their breakup and notes that "distance definitely does not help."
"I was feeling very insecure about our age difference and everything. There were a couple sort of moments that happened where I needed his reassurance and didn't really get it," she says.
After breaking up, the former couple didn't talk for "30 to 60 days" and were "very hostile" with each other.
"I think that always comes from a place of hurt when you really care for someone," Theresa says.
Flash forward to Thanksgiving and the exes began talking again as friends.
"I think we both really care about each other still, and we will always care about each other. We went through this experience together and it was authentic," Theresa says.
Looking ahead, Theresa says she's not sure what the future holds.
"It could go either way, but I think as of right now I'm pretty confident in saying we're not together. I don't know that we will get back together," she says.
Theresa's Approach to Dating Now
This wasn’t the first time Theresa has dated a younger man, hence her reservations about other people’s opinions about her relationship.
“I had had that scrutiny of the double standard and everything, and so I was kind of preparing myself for that,” she explains.
Instead, Theresa has been pleasantly surprised by people’s reactions to the show.

“I’m not getting as much hate over it as I had expected. And I don’t know if that’s because they could see so much of the authenticity in my relationship with John. But I’m so happy that I’m experiencing what it is, which is women are being more empowered, and I’m just so happy about it,” she says.
Even Theresa’s own children weren’t phased by the age difference.

“They didn’t really react very much. My youngest was the only one that really was like, ‘Oh my God.’ I mean, they all had that, ‘Oh my God’ sort of initial reaction because I mean, how could you not?’” she says.
Theresa does say that her youngest son has been “struggling” a little bit with the “optics” of her televised relationship with John.

“His friends from college, and I think some of them from high school are sending him messages, maybe teasing him a little bit. His mom is this like quasi-famous person now in this weird sort of dynamic, and so he just is trying to figure out how to navigate it,” she says.
After breaking up with John, Theresa hasn’t dated anyone else. In fact, she’s been intentional about staying single for the time being.
“I put a hold on dating, since I just didn’t know what was going to happen with the show. I just didn’t have it in me either, honestly,” she explains.
Still, that doesn’t mean she isn’t dreaming of finding someone special one day.
“I really want to find my person. I will find my person,” she says.

title: ‘Summer House’ Star Kyle Cooke Breaks Silence About West Wilson, Amanda Batula Relationship
summary: “Summer House” star Kyle Cooke spoke out about his ex-wife Amanda Batula's relationship with West Wilson, if he's spoken to Ciara Miller and more.
story: “Summer House” star Kyle Cooke is speaking out after his ex-wife Amanda Batula and West Wilson, who are both his co-stars on the Bravo show, publicly confirmed their romance.

Batula and Wilson released a joint statement on March 31 on their respective Instagram stories in which they addressed speculation about their connection. “We’ve shown up for each other as friends over the years, through all the highs and lows, and what’s developed recently was the last thing either of us expected,” they wrote, in part. “Our connection grew out of a genuine, long-standing friendship which made it especially important for us to approach this with care. As our feelings evolved, we wanted to take time to understand exactly what we were feeling.”
Their statement and romance comes after Batula and Cooke, who tied the knot in September 2021, announced in January that they were separating, People reported at the time.

On April 1, Cooke spoke to the host of the “So Bad It’s Good With Ryan Bailey” podcast on the streets of New York and revealed how he is feeling about the “Summer House” scandal surrounding Batula and Wilson.
When asked how he is doing, Cooke replied, “I’m good. You know, I appreciate people checking in. I’m actually really worried about Amanda if I’m being honest.”
He said he spoke to her the night before she released the joint statement with Wilson and she gave him a heads up about the post. He also chatted with his estranged wife the following night.
“I understand people have all sorts of opinions, and I’m not justifying any behavior. But like, from what I’m seeing, she’s kind of getting cyber bullied,” Cooke shared.

He added, “All I know is West seems to be the kind of guy playing multiple women at the same time. Somehow, in all of this, like 50,000 people have decided that West deserves their follow. Meanwhile, they’re just lighting up Amanda. I just feel bad because, like, Amanda knows that what she did was wrong, and she’s trying to come to terms with it, but she is not well.”
Cooke revealed that he realized his ex’s relationship with Wilson wasn’t strictly platonic over the weekend.
But, he said he doesn’t know the exact timeline of the relationship.

“It definitely makes me second guess a lot of things last summer, at a minimum. Just how checked out she was. In my own unsuccessful ways, I was still trying to put an effort in. I mean, it’s a mental mind f— for me.”
Cooke and “Summer House” fans are currently watching the dynamic between Batula and Wilson unfold as Season 10 of the Bravo reality series, which was filmed last summer, is currently airing.
He told Ryan Bailey that he also received “some half-a---ed text message” from Wilson, who joined the show in 2024, about the situation. He later referred to the message as “word salad.”
Cooke then reiterated that he is concerned with how fans of the show are responding to the scandal. He advised viewers to reconsider their reactions.

“Amanda deals with mental health issues,” he noted. “Before cyber bullying her and then giving West a follow, maybe think twice … we’re still trying to figure all this out. I’m still trying to figure out the details. But I know I can imagine West is using the same charm that he’s used on just about every other chick.”

Cooke also called out Wilson for his behavior on the recent episode of “Summer House” Season 10 and for his comments during an appearance on “Watch What Happens Live” last week. At the time, Wilson said he and Batula “had just been hanging out” and referred to her as his friend.
Speaking more about his reaction to the news, Cooke said he is “frustrated” given that a “Summer House” spinoff, titled “In the City,” is set to premiere soon.

“There’s so much that gets unpacked there,” he said about the new series. “I’m obviously disappointed in her, but just hearing her and hearing how unwell she is, you know dark thoughts, I just ask people to just maybe let off the gas a little bit with the cyber bullying and hate.”

He confirmed that he has been in contact with fellow “Summer House” star Ciara Miller, as well. Miller and Wilson had an on-and-off relationship with Wilson for years, and Season 10 shows that the two were still flirtatious. Additionally, Miller and Batula have been close friends for years.
“Ciara and I have been in touch this whole week,” Cooke said.

title: Megan Thee Stallion Hospitalized After Falling ‘Very Ill’ Mid-Performance of ‘Moulin Rouge’
summary: Megan Thee Stallion left the March 31 performance of Broadway’s “Moulin Rouge! The Musical" after getting sick and having to go a hospital.
story: Megan Thee Stallion left the March 31 performance of Broadway’s “Moulin Rouge! The Musical” midway through the show after she fell ill, a spokesperson for the rapper said.

“During Tuesday night’s production, Megan started feeling very ill and was promptly transported to a local hospital, where her symptoms are currently being evaluated," her spokesperson said in a statement.
Her spokesperson said in a follow-up statement April 1 that the rapper's doctors "identified extreme exhaustion, dehydration, vasoconstriction and low metabolic levels as the cause of her symptoms."
"Megan has since been treated, discharged and is now resting," her rep said.
Megan Thee Stallion will return to the Broadway stage on Thursday, April 2, according to her rep.
On Instagram, the rapper called her health scare "a real wake-up call."

"I’ve been pushing myself past my limits lately, running on empty, and my body finally said enough," she said April 1. "It honestly scared me. I thought I was gonna faint on stage, I really tried to push through my performance but I just couldn’t."
She said that after a day to rest and reset, she'll be back on stage "stronger, clearer, and ready to give you 100% the way you deserve."

The “WAP” hitmaker announced in February that she would be joining the show as Harold Zidler for a limited run starting March 24 and ending May 17. It marks the first time a person identifying as female played the character. Zidler is the owner of the Moulin Rouge nightclub, and the character was first brought to life by Jim Broadbent in the 2001 film.
“Thee hottest news on Broadway❣️🎩” the rapper captioned a post revealing news of her Broadway debut on Instagram.
“Stepping onto the Broadway stage and joining the ‘Moulin Rouge! The Musical’ team is an absolute honor,” Megan Thee Stallion said about joining the show in a statement at the time, per Variety.

“I’ve always believed in pushing myself creatively and theater is definitely a new opportunity that I’m excited to embrace. Broadway demands a different level of discipline, preparation and storytelling, but I’m up for the challenge and can’t wait for the Hotties to see a new side of me.”
“Moulin Rouge! The Musical” is scheduled to conclude its seven-year Broadway run on July 26.

title: 'The Masked Singer' Finale To Pause Mid-Show For Trump's Presidential Address. What Time Will It Be Back?
summary: President Donald Trump announced he would be delivering a rare primetime address to the nation on April 1, interrupting "The Masked Singer" finale. Here's when it'll resume.
story: President Donald Trump announced he would be delivering a rare primetime address to the nation on April 1 to discuss the war with Iran, which will pause several primetime broadcasts.

"The Masked Singer" was set to air its two-hour Season 14 finale at 8 p.m./7 p.m. CT on Wednesday, April 1, on Fox. The president's address will start at 9 p.m. ET and air on major broadcast networks.
How can viewers catch up on the rest of the "Masked Singer" season finale? We're sharing everything you need to know, including the other shows that will be impacted by the presidential address.
The 'Masked Singer' Will Be Interrupted Halfway Through the Season Finale
Anyone tuning in to watch the Season 14 finale of "The Masked Singer" will have to bring their patience.
Per Deadline, the episode will be paused at 9 p.m. ET to air the 20-minute presidential address on Fox. The finale will then resume once again and finish around 10:20 p.m. ET.
The episode will stream in its entirety on Hulu on April 2.
Local news will air on Fox following "The Masked Singer."
What Other Shows Are Impacted by the Presidential Address?
"The Masked Singer" isn't the only primetime show to be interrupted by the presidential address. Here's a list of a few other shows that will be impacted.
"Survivor" was set to air a two-hour episode from 8-10 p.m. ET. on April 1.

Per Deadline, the network plans to pause the episode at 9 p.m. when the presidential address begins and resume it afterwards. Similar to "The Masked Singer," the two-hour episode of "Survivor" will end around 10:20 p.m. ET.
Afterwards, CBS will air a new episode of "America's Culinary Cup" at 10:20 p.m. ET, which was supposed to start at 10 p.m. ET.
Subsequently, "The Late Show" will be pushed back to 11:50 p.m. ET, following local news at 11:20 p.m.
A new episode of "Chicago Med' will air uninterrupted from 8-9 p.m., per Deadline. However, "Chicago Fire" will start at 9:20 p.m. ET after the presidential address, instead of 9 p.m. ET.
Afterwards, "Chicago P.D." will begin at 10:20 p.m. ET. instead of 10 p.m. ET.
Local news will begin at 11:20 p.m. ET, followed by "The Tonight Show" at 11:50 pm. ET.

Per Deadline, ABC is replacing new episodes of "The Greatest Average American" and "Shark Tank" with repeats on both coasts. The 9 p.m. rerun of “The Greatest Average American” will be interrupted by the presidential address and will resume in progress.
Since ABC isn't airing new episodes of its 9 and 10 p.m. shows, the network will resume regularly scheduled programming (local news and a rerun of "Jimmy Kimmel Live") at 11 p.m. ET.

title: Jen Shah Reveals What Led Her to Plead Guilty To Conspiracy to Commit Wire Fraud
summary: “Real Housewives of Salt Lake City” star Jen Shah said she "made wrong decisions" leading to her arrest in connection with her participation in a nationwide telemarketing fraud scheme.
story: Nearly four months after she was released early from her 6.5-year prison sentence, “Real Housewives of Salt Lake City” star Jen Shah said she "made wrong decisions" leading to her arrest in connection with her participation in a nationwide telemarketing fraud scheme.
Shah, who is currently serving the rest of her sentence under home confinement, spoke to People in her first interview since her prison release and apologized.

"I was wrong," she said. "I made wrong decisions. I should have done things differently. I should have been more diligent. And I’m deeply remorseful and sorry for my actions and for my part. I take full responsibility."
Why Did Jen Shah Go to Prison?

In 2021, Shah was arrested in connection with her alleged participation in a telemarketing fraud scheme. The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Southern District of New York said the scheme defrauded hundreds of people out of money starting in 2012, per a news release published at the time.
Shah and her assistant, Stuart Smith, were each charged with one count of conspiracy to commit wire fraud and one count of conspiracy to commit money laundering, the office said.

Shah initially denied any wrongdoing, but in 2022, she pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud in connection with telemarketing, per NBC News. As part of her plea deal, Shah was ordered to forfeit $6.5 million and pay restitution up to $9.5 million, per a 2022 news release.
In 2023, a judge sentenced the reality star to 78 months in federal prison and five years of supervision upon her release.
Shah reported to prison in February 2023. The following month, her prison sentence was reduced by one year.

In December 2025, Bureau of Prisons spokesperson Emery Nelson told NBC News that Shah had been released from federal prison more than four years early from her 6.5-year sentence and transferred to either home confinement or a halfway house. Nelson declined to share her exact location for privacy and safety reasons at the time.

In her interview with People, Shah confirmed that she had been wearing an ankle monitor while under home confinement for the last 90 days. The reality star said she expects to be on home confinement until around August 2026.
Jen Shah Addresses Guilty Plea

According to the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the United States District Court of the Southern District of New York,“the Superseding Indictment, and statements made during the plea and other proceedings in the case” alleged that Shah and Smith “engaged in a widespread, coordinated effort to traffic in lists of potential victims, or ‘leads.”
The indictment described how victims were sold “business services.” When she pleaded guilty to one count of fraud in 2022, Shah said these services “had little to no value.”
Speaking to People about her involvement in the telemarketing scheme, Shah said she had limited understanding of the inner workings of the operation.

“It’s a long and a very complex journey that brought me to this point,” she said. “And without re-litigating it, I became involved in the case because I made horrible business decisions and I disregarded huge red flags. I allowed the lines to be blurred between personal friendships and ethical business practices. And in essence, I trusted the wrong people at a very vulnerable time in my life.”
Shah said she thought she was “doing the right thing for the majority of the time.”

“What happened was down the line, people that I worked with were working with a lot of other people,” she said. “Once that initial fulfillment was happening, things were happening beyond the point of sale with that customer that I didn’t know about.”
Shah said she wanted to take accountability for her actions.

“It can happen if you’re not careful, if you’re not being diligent and you’re not paying attention to the red flags,” she said. “But you have a responsibility once you’re in that position to make sure it doesn’t.”

Shah also explained why she pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud. She said she made the decision around July 2022 after prosecutors sent her legal team a large amount of evidence with just a few weeks to go before her trial began.
"It was like a train hit,” Shah said. "That was the first time I saw all of it — the communications, the interviews, the witnesses."

"I saw for the first time that there were people who were hurt," she added. "That there were actual victims as a result of this conspiracy. I had never seen anything with my own eyes. That changed things for me.
Shah said she is now working to pay back the restitution she owes, which totals more than $6 million.
"I’m sorry," she said. "I’m accepting responsibility, and I’ve made it my mission to make sure that people are paid back."

title: Dan Levy Calls Death of Catherine O’Hara a ‘Collective Loss,’ Shares What Gives Him ‘Comfort’ Now
summary: Dan Levy appeared on "The Tonight Show" and spoke glowingly of "Schitt's Creek" co-star Catherine O'Hara following her death.
story: Dan Levy has found a source of "great comfort" amid the death of his "Schitt's Creek" co-star and on-screen mom, Catherine O’Hara.
Levy, who played David Rose on “Schitt’s Creek,” opened up about O'Hara's death on the March 31 episode of “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon.”

“It’s like a collective loss, I think. She was the greatest,” he said. “She’s irreplaceable, and I think the great comfort for me has just been to see how loved she was, you know what I mean? The outpouring. Everyone felt like they kind of knew her."
“She was one of the funniest comedians I’ve ever seen,” Fallon said.
“Unbelievably talented at improvising. One of the great, great, great, great queens,” Levy added.

O’Hara died in January at the age of 71 of a pulmonary embolism after having rectal cancer. She was beloved for her role as Moira on “Schitt’s Creek,” playing the eccentric matriarch of the Rose family with an untraceable accent that became her calling card.

“The accent was kind of up to me,” she told TODAY’s Willie Geist during a Sunday Sitdown interview in 2024. “The accents, I should say. When people try to imitate that character, the mistake most people make is to be consistent.”
O’Hara was a comedy icon with 10 Emmy nominations and two wins, one for best actress in a comedy series for “Schitt’s Creek” and another for her writing on “SCTV Network.”

O’Hara also starred in the first two “Home Alone” movies, “Best in Show,” “Beetlejuice” (and its 2024 sequel, “Beetlejuice, Beetlejuice"), “The Nightmare Before Christmas,” “The Last of Us” and, most recently, Apple TV’s “The Studio," which got her an Emmy nomination for supporting actress in a comedy series.

In March, O’Hara posthumously won an Actor Award for outstanding performance by a female actor in a comedy series for her work on “The Studio.” Co-star Seth Rogen raved about her when he accepted it on her behalf.

“Something that I’ve just been marveling at over the last few weeks was really her ability to be generous and kind and gracious while never, ever minimizing her own talents and her own ability to contribute to the work that we were doing,” he said.
“She knew she could destroy, and she wanted to destroy every day on set.”

title: EXCLUSIVE: 'Baby-Sitters Club' Author Shares a New Book Coming Out for 40-Year Anniversary
summary: Four decades since the first book of “The Baby-Sitters Club” series came out, creator and author Ann M. Martin is looking back.“When I set out to write the first book in The Baby-Sitters Club, 'Kristy’s Great Idea,' I wanted to create a series about girls who were strong, entrepreneurial, independent thinkers, and problem-solvers — and friends who were kind, compassionate, and supportive of one another despite their many differences."Now, nearly four decades later, I receive letters fr
story: Four decades since the first book of “The Baby-Sitters Club” series came out, creator and author Ann M. Martin is looking back.

“When I set out to write the first book in The Baby-Sitters Club, 'Kristy’s Great Idea,' I wanted to create a series about girls who were strong, entrepreneurial, independent thinkers, and problem-solvers — and friends who were kind, compassionate, and supportive of one another despite their many differences.

"Now, nearly four decades later, I receive letters from kids who say their parents introduced them to the series and that they enjoy reading the books together. I love the idea of parents, caregivers, and children bonding over the stories," she says.
The first "Baby-sitters Club" book led to a total of 250 books, 200 million copies sold, shows on HBO and Netflix, a film and now, another graphic novel addition.

As part of the book series’ 40-year anniversary, Scholastic is releasing the first-ever graphic novel adaptation of a “Baby-sitters Club” super special book. The 15 “super special” books are longer than the standard novels and feature narration from every member of the BSC.
“The Baby-sitters Club Super Special: Baby-sitters on Board,” adapted and illustrated by Raina Telgemeier and Gale Galligan, comes out on Sept. 15.

In the book, Kristy, Claudia, Mary Anne, Stacey, Dawn, Mallory and Jessi head to the Bahamas on a cruise for three days of fun. Amid all the adventure, these girls still have time for what they do best: babysitting.

“The graphic novel adaptations are a wonderful addition to the series,” Martin said in a statement to TODAY.com. “I’m in awe of how a story can be carried forward through illustrations — even by something as seemingly simple as a facial expression.”
New York Times bestselling authors Raina Telgemeier and Gale Galligan, who previously collaborated on BSC graphic novels, say the opportunity feels like a full-circle moment.
Telgemeier told TODAY.com in a statement that she "fell in love" with the BSC in 1986.

"My fondness for the girls and the world of Stoneybrook is as strong as ever," Telgemeier reveals. "What a joy to pick up my pencil and return to illustrating Ann’s characters, and what a thrill to share this ride with Gale — who is such a force of cartooning nature themself!"
Galligan, too, shares a personal connection to the BSC storyline and one character in particular.

“From the moment I met Claudia Kishi and discovered that there were books about Asian American kids like me, to the moment I saw my own graphic novel adaptation on a bookshelf for the first time, 'The Baby-sitters Club' has opened my life up in ways I could never have imagined," Galligan said.
"I’m delighted to celebrate its 40th anniversary in the most BSC way possible: by collaborating with dear friends," Galligan added.
Martin echoes the book' sisterhood. She says "stepping into the world of Stoneybrook" with this book feels like a "reunion with close friends."

"As we approach the 40-year anniversary of The Baby-Sitters Club, it’s deeply meaningful to know that the series has helped inspire a love of reading," Martin said. "I am humbled, honored, and incredibly grateful.”
Take a Sneak Peek at the 1st 'Baby-sitters Club' Super Special Graphic Novel
“The Baby-sitters Club Super Super Special: Baby-sitters on Board!” is the series’ first super special graphic novel.

"The Baby-sitters Club boards the Ocean Princess for a cruise to the Bahamas and Disney World! Mary Anne meets a mysterious girl who never quite tells the truth. Claudia gets mixed up with a secret admirer. Kristy and Stacey make special friends, and Dawn has a fabulous romance," the official synopsis reads.

title: Why Amanda Batula, West Wilson’s Confirmed Romance Is Shocking Fans of ‘Summer House’
summary: Longtime "Summer House" co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson have confirmed they are dating after rampant speculation, fueling an already dramatic Season 10 release
story: Longtime "Summer House" co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson have confirmed they are dating after rampant speculation, fueling an already dramatic Season 10 release.
The new couple shocked fans after revealing their secret romance in a joint statement March 31 on their respective Instagram stories.
“We’ve seen the growing online speculation, so while this is still very new, we wanted to provide some clarity,” the statement, which Batula and Wilson individually posted, said.

“It was never our intention to purposely hide anything. Given the complicated relationship dynamics involved and the scrutiny that comes with being on a reality show, we needed a little space to process things privately before speaking on it," they continued.
Confirmation about the Bravo stars' romance comes amid weeks of online speculation about the nature of their relationship following Batula's split from estranged husband Kyle Cooke in January.
The rumors also hint of possible tension between Batula and close friend and co-star Ciara Miller, who had an on-and-off romance with Wilson for years.
Who Are West Wilson and Amanda Batula?

Batula and Wilson are both current stars of Bravo's "Summer House." Batula has appeared on the show since Season 1, and the show documented the beginning of her relationship with Cooke, whom she married in 2021.
Wilson joined the show in Season 8, which premiered in 2024.
Their joint statement March 31 confirming their romance comes on the heels of Batula’s split from Cooke, which they announced on Instagram in January.

“After much reflection, we have mutually and amicably decided to part ways as a couple,” Batula and Cooke wrote, per People. “We share this with a heavy heart and kindly ask for your grace and support while we focus on our personal growth and healing.”
Batula and Cooke's marital struggles have featured prominently on "Summer House," including a Season 3 confession from Cooke that he had cheated on Batula.

Meanwhile, Wilson has been involved for several years in an on-and-off dynamic with Batula’s friend Ciara Miller, another castmate. Batula expressed gratitude for Miller's support during her breakup during an interview with Marie Claire published in March 2026.

"It would have been a very difficult summer to get through without Ciara. She is one of the kindest, most loving, loyal friends I’ve ever had. She sends me texts out of the blue, reminding me how strong, powerful, smart, and kind I am — I don’t think she even realizes how much I need those texts," Batula said.

Miller and Wilson started dating while filming Season 8 in the summer of 2023 before breaking up that December. But the sparks never fully faded. In Season 10, which is currently airing and was filmed in the summer of 2025, Wilson and Miller continue to flirt, with the door seemingly open to a rekindling.
In Batula's March interview, she also revealed Wilson had also helped her during her separation from Cooke, saying Wilson was "supportive" in "ways (she) wasn’t even aware of when filming."
"It’s not performative; it’s just really authentic to who West is. He checks Kyle and puts him in his place, and you’ll continue to see him be there for me throughout the season," Batula said.
What Did Amanda Batula and West Wilson Say in the Joint Statement?
Batula and Wilson explained in their March 31 statement their “connection” developed from a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”
“We’ve shown up for each other as friends over the years, through all the highs and lows, and what’s developed recently was the last thing either of us expected,” they wrote.

“As our feelings evolved, we wanted to take time to understand exactly what we were feeling,” they added. “We also recognize that this has had an impact beyond just us and never wanted our actions to cause any hurt or be perceived as careless.”
Just days before releasing the statement, Wilson denied the rumors involving him and Batula on "Watch What Happens Live." He said they had "just been hanging out."
"If it's not clear, that's a very important person to me, and I care about her a lot," he said on March 24. "That's a friend. Hope it's clear."
Have Ciara Miller or Kyle Cooke Reacted?
TODAY.com has reached out to reps for Batula, Wilson, Miller and Cooke for comment.
While neither Miller nor Cooke has commented publicly on the confirmed "connection" between their exes, as of March 31, Miller does not follow Wilson and Batula on Instagram.

Miller's first Instagram post since the release of Wilson and Batula's statement was of her interviewing the stars of "Your Friends & Neighbors" on the red carpet of its Season 2 premiere on March 30.
In one clip from her interviews, Miller asked show star Jon Hamm how he's feeling.
"Wonderful, thank you, how are you?" Hamm said.
"I'm good. Well, I've been better," Miller responded.

Before their statement, Cooke was asked about the speculation regarding Wilson and Batula on "Watch What Happens Live" March 10. He called the rumors "outrageous" and said, "I don't think there's any merit to it."
Asked how he'd react if it was true, Cooke said, "It would certainly catch me by surprise and feel a little reckless, and I think I'd probably be the last person people would be worried about.
"I think Ciara would probably have something to say," he added. "But if it made Amanda happy, I think I would just have to vote yay."
What's Happening with 'Summer House'?
"Summer House" Season 10 is currently airing. New episodes air on Tuesdays at 8 p.m. Episode 9 aired on March 31.
After Wilson and Batula released their statement, Bravo's Andy Cohen confirmed the Season 10 cast reunion has not yet been filmed.
"My team is getting ready for THE SUMMER HOUSE REUNION! Send me all of your questions for the cast NOW and let me know where you’re from in the question!" he posted on X March 31.

title: Read With Jenna's April 2026 Is an 'Extraordinary Book' That Will 'Open Hearts and Change Minds'
summary: Read With Jenna's April 2026 pick is "Upward Bound" by Woody Brown.
story: Jenna Bush Hager says she was “so enthralled” by “Upward Bound,” her April 2026 book club pick, that she read it on a single airplane ride, “unable to tear myself away from the beauty in its pages.”
"Upward Bound" is Woody Brown's debut novel. Brown is autistic and nonverbal. With the help of his mom, Mary Brown, he uses a letter board to communicate and write.

"He has written an extraordinary story about what becomes possible when even one person believes in you. Set in an adult day care center called Upward Bound, we meet an unforgettable cast of characters — Carlos, Mariana, Jorge, Tom, Ann, and, at the heart of it all, Walter, who is only truly understood by his mother," Jenna says.
"This is a love story about mothers and sons — a window into a world so often misunderstood. I am confident it will open hearts and change minds," she continues.
Looking ahead to the release of his first novel, Brown, sitting next to his mother Mary Brown, tells TODAY.com he feels "nervous and excited."
"Upward Bound" by Woody Brown
"I knew that this story needed to be told, and thanks to my education and excellent mentors, I was in a uniquely apt position to tell it the right way," he says.
"How many like me are trapped in bodies, locked in silence, and yearning for education and true connection?"
In a taped interview with Jenna that aired on TODAY with Jenna and Sheinelle on March 31, Mary Brown said she was told her son was intellectually disabled and would never graduate high school.

Instead of accepting that, Mary Brown learned how to use a letter board. Together, they followed his high school curriculum through to his graduation in 2017. When she realized his next step was adult day care, Mary Brown quit her job so they could both attend UCLA.
"To finally be in the room where the learning was happening ... I felt like I was in heaven," Woody Brown told Jenna.
"We came to realize that he had real potential for a productive and happy life," Mary Brown said.
"Upward Bound" is the start, but not the end, of Woody Brown's writing ambitions — he's already on Chapter 6 of his next book.

Woody Brown tells TODAY.com he hopes readers take away two things from his debut: “First, that someone like me can be a good writer and want to read my next novel. Second, I hope that people will question their assumptions about the intelligence of profoundly disabled people,” he says.

His debut features an array of characters in the adult daycare center, including Tom, who has both cerebral palsy and a crush on the lifeguard, and Carlos, the employee who has a tender connection with Jorge, a nonspeaking client with a penchant for running away.
The book sometimes veers into dark places. "The tragedy is real," Woody Brown says. "So not to be honest about it would be unjust to the characters."
He also has a message for his younger self. "Your instincts are correct. You just have to wait for everybody else to catch up. Just keep learning, because a writer needs a good education," he says.

title: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page Are Living La Bella Vita on the Cover of Essence
summary: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page are the cover stars for Essence's latest edition. The two star in the upcoming rom-com "You, Me & Tuscany."
story: Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page are showing off all their best angles as the cover stars of Essence's latest digital edition.
TODAY.com is exclusively revealing the actors’ stunning cover and photo spread ahead of the release of their upcoming rom-com, “You, Me & Tuscany.”
In the photos, the two look quite dashing while posing amid the Italian architecture. Naturally, a red moped made an appearance, too.

Out April 10, “You, Me & Tuscany” follows Anna (Bailey), a young woman who is down on her luck after losing her job. She meets Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor), a handsome Italian man who happens to own a villa that sits empty in Tuscany.

Inspired and with no place to call home, Anna decides to go to Matteo’s villa without him knowing. There, she becomes entangled in a web of lies and meets Matteo’s swoon-worthy cousin Michael (Page). As feelings grow between the two, the lies Anna has told could negatively impact their future.

“I am in my very grown woman era,” Bailey tells Essence in the accompanying cover story. “This is the first project that I am showing myself in my full adulthood. And I loved it because it reflects the times that I’m in right now.”
Not only is this a stepping stone for the singer-turned-actor after starring in “The Little Mermaid” and “The Color Purple,” but it’s also a project that made her proud to celebrate Black love.

“It was so important for me to be a part of a project where I could see myself on-screen, where I could see Black love on-screen,” Bailey says. “A film where I can see us figuring it out in a joyful way.”
Produced by Will Packer, the man behind films like “Think Like a Man” and “Girls Trip,” he found the perfect stars and helped bring the joyful love to life alongside director Kat Coiro.

“And that’s very much the journey the characters are on,” he says. “Letting your co-star unlock the pieces of you that can trust someone else, and that can lean into the pieces of you that you’re holding back.”

He adds, “This movie is very much about learning to trust each other. When you’re doing romantic stories, it’s about building an emotional trust with each other, knowing that you can go to places where you are expressing very, very vulnerable things.”

title: The Kelces Just Got Their 5th Girl, and She’s a Dog! What They Named Her
summary: The Kelce family has added another girl to the mix … an adorable new puppy named Bernadette.
story: The Kelce family has added another girl to the mix … an adorable new puppy.

Kylie Kelce revealed in the latest episode of her “FAFO” series, an extension of her “Not Gonna Lie” podcast, that six weeks ago she worked with the Pennsylvania Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals’ Main Line Animal Rescue to find a four-legged friend for their household.

“Jason (Kelce) does know that I’m here today,” Kylie Kelce said at the start of the video. “We did discuss whether or not he would be peeved if I came home with an animal. And when I said rate on a scale of one to 10, he said, ‘10.’”
But, Kylie Kelce cheekily added, her husband didn’t clarify “which side of the scale was pissed.”
Throughout the episode, the mother of four met multiple friendly dogs and cats while also learning about PSPCA’s rescue efforts and how the organization cares for animals.

Toward the end of the episode, Kylie Kelce sat in a dog pen surrounded by eight-week-old German shepherd puppies. She mused over which one had a personality that would be the perfect fit for the Kelces.
She also already had a name picked out: Bernadette.
“Are you a Bernadette? Tell me the truth,” she said to one puppy. She then turned to the camera and said, “Look at her little dimple where her little whiskers are."
She knew that the puppy in her arms was the one she wanted to adopt.

“I can’t help myself! Someone let me loose in a rescue and this is what happened,” Kylie Kelce said. “I met some very cute cats. But, like I’ve said a million times at this point, we’re not ready for that. When I am ready for that, I’m going to march my a-- right back to the PSPCA and I’m going to find me a cat that wants to come home and live at the Kelce household.”
She continued, “But for now … may I introduce Bernadette Kelce!”
